title: Office data routine
story_function: Establish the future data office and the protagonist's repetitive upload work.
image_prompt:
Office data routine. Establish the future data office and the protagonist's repetitive upload work. Create a still image that can become the first frame of this shot. Continuity: same exhausted protagonist, same bureaucratic boss when present, same black CIVILIZATION ARCHIVE hard drive when present, cold blue data-office palette before the impact, ash-gray ruin palette after the impact, blue holographic reconstruction light in the final sequence. Style: vertical 9:16 cinematic frame, photorealistic sci-fi disaster film style, controlled composition, clear subject hierarchy, no subtitles, no watermark. Story context: # The Last Data

A future architectural data worker believes that scanning and uploading civilization data is meaningless routine work. In a cold data office, the protagonist is pressured by a boss to keep uploading architectural scan data even as an extinction-level impact alert interrupts the workday.

The conflict escalates because the boss still demands that the data upload be completed before evacuation. The protagonist finally breaks down, asking what the work is for if the world is ending.

After the catastrophe, the protagonist wakes in the ruins of the city. The office, the buildings, and the old civilization appear destroyed. In the ruins, the protagonist discovers a hard drive marked `CIVILIZATION ARCHIVE`.

When the hard drive activates, the stored architectural data becomes the basis for rebuilding the city. Blue holographic scans and architectural wireframes rise from the ruins, suggesting that the data once considered meaningless has become the last surviving blueprint of civilization.
video_prompt: Office data routine. Slow push-in through the future data office; screens flicker subtly while the protagonist repeats the upload routine. Preserve character and environment continuity. Use smooth, physically plausible camera motion. Do not add unsupported characters, logos, subtitles, or unrelated props.
